Class WaitlistController

java.lang.Object
com.example.eventparticipation.WaitlistController
public class WaitlistController extends Object
Controller handling Waitlist business logic, including the Lottery system.

Manages the state transitions of entrants within an event's waitlist subcollection. Contains the logic for randomly sampling entrants (The Lottery) and drawing replacement applicants.

Relevant user stories:

  • US 01.04.03 Opt out of receiving notifications
  • US 02.05.02 Sample a specified number of attendees (Lottery)
  • US 02.05.03 Draw a replacement applicant

    Method
    Description
    com.google.android.gms.tasks.Task<String>
    drawReplacement(String eventId)
    Draws a single replacement applicant from the waiting pool if a spot opens up.
    com.google.android.gms.tasks.Task<Void>
    runLottery(String eventId, int sampleSize)
    Runs the lottery to randomly select a specified number of entrants from the waiting pool.

  • Constructor Details

    • WaitlistController

      public WaitlistController()
      Initializes the controller with the default Firestore instance.

    • WaitlistController

      public WaitlistController(com.google.firebase.firestore.FirebaseFirestore injectedDb)
      Initializes the controller with a provided Firestore instance. Useful for dependency injection during unit testing.
      Parameters:
      injectedDb - The Firestore instance to use.

  • Method Details

    • runLottery

      public com.google.android.gms.tasks.Task<Void> runLottery(String eventId, int sampleSize)
      Runs the lottery to randomly select a specified number of entrants from the waiting pool.
      Parameters:
      eventId - The ID of the event.
      sampleSize - The number of entrants to select.
      Returns:
      A Task that resolves when the batch update completes.

    • drawReplacement

      public com.google.android.gms.tasks.Task<String> drawReplacement(String eventId)
      Draws a single replacement applicant from the waiting pool if a spot opens up.
      Parameters:
      eventId - The ID of the event.
      Returns:
      A Task that resolves to the ID of the newly selected entrant, or null if empty.
